summary: Completed matching decompilation coverage for Luigi's fighter specials by adding C implementations for Fireball, Green Missile, Super Jump Punch, and Luigi Cyclone, wiring those new objects into the build, expanding Luigi headers/action-state metadata, and deleting most remaining Luigi assembly. The PR also made small supporting fixes to common fighter helpers, Luigi-specific struct naming, item-kind usage, and demo-motion struct declarations. There were no PR body comments or review comments in the slice, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from code changes.
what_changed: Added new matched C source files for Luigi's neutral special Fireball, side special Green Missile, up special Super Jump Punch, and down special Luigi Cyclone.
Reduced the remaining Luigi assembly in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftluigi.s substantially, consistent with moving those routines into C.
Added ftLuigi special object files to obj_files.mk so the new C implementations are linked before the remaining Luigi assembly.

Expanded ftluigi.h from a minimal header into a full Luigi special header with ftLuigiAction enum values, action-state flag macros, named ftLuigiAttributes fields, and prototypes for SpecialN/SpecialS/SpecialHi/SpecialLw callbacks.
Changed Luigi OnLoad from raw item kind 0x69U to It_Kind_Luigi_Fire.
Added func_80142640 to resolve Luigi demo-motion symbol pointers via lbl_803D0AAC and added DemoMotionSymbols/DemoMotionFiles structs in common_structs.h.
Renamed SpecialAttrs_Luigi offset 0x222C from a raw u32 x222C to BOOL x222C_cycloneCharge and used it in Luigi Cyclone logic.
Changed func_8007CC78 signature/order from func_8007CC78(f32 max, Fighter* fp) to func_8007CC78(Fighter* fp, f32 max), updating callers in Donkey, Mario, fighter.c, and ftcommon.h.
Added an extern declaration for EnvColl_CheckGroundAndLedge in ftGameWatch_SpecialHi.c and touched ftgamewatch.h/ftgamewatch.s, apparently as collateral matching/header normalization.
smart_moves: Split Luigi's completed special-move decomp into separate files by move suffix: ftLuigi_SpecialN.c, ftLuigi_SpecialS.c, ftLuigi_SpecialHi.c, and ftLuigi_SpecialLw.c, mirroring fighter-special organization used elsewhere.
Replaced magic action IDs with AS_LUIGI_* enum constants and replaced a magic Luigi fire item kind with It_Kind_Luigi_Fire, improving future auditability without changing behavior.
Captured action-state flag bitmasks in named FTLUIGI_* macros, making Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC calls easier to compare and reuse.

Named the previously raw Luigi special-attributes layout by move family, preserving offsets while documenting gameplay meaning such as GREENMISSILE_MISFIRE_CHANCE, SUPERJUMP_LANDING_LAG, and CYCLONE_TAP_MOMENTUM.
Used small inline helper routines in the Cyclone implementation for repeated setup patterns such as SetVars, SetCall, SetGFX, and callback nulling.
Corrected func_8007CC78's prototype so the Fighter* parameter is first, then updated all known callsites visible in the diff.
decomp_lessons: When completing a fighter, isolate each special move into its own translation unit and add only those .c.o files to obj_files.mk while leaving any not-yet-decompiled assembly file in place.
Move-specific headers are valuable once a fighter reaches special-move coverage: define action-state enums, flags, attributes, and callback prototypes in one character-local header.
Naming raw special-attribute offsets by move and behavior can clarify matching code without changing layout; keep the original xNN offset prefix in field names to preserve offset traceability.
Use enum constants and item-kind symbols instead of raw numeric literals once the surrounding headers expose them; this reduces later reverse-engineering ambiguity.
If a helper prototype is discovered to have arguments in the wrong order, fix the declaration and every visible callsite in the same PR to avoid silent mismatches.
For fighter command variables and state vars, names like x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2340_stateVar1, x2344_stateVar2, and character-specific x222C_cycloneCharge help bridge assembly offsets and C intent.
Accessory and hitlag callbacks are part of matching behavior for specials: examples include x21BC_callback_Accessory4 for Luigi Fireball spawning and x21D4/x21D8 pause/resume callbacks for effects.
naming_conventions: Luigi action states use AS_LUIGI_SPECIALN, AS_LUIGI_SPECIALAIRN, AS_LUIGI_SPECIALS_START/HOLD/FLY/END/LAUNCH/MISFIRE, AS_LUIGI_SPECIALAIRS_*, AS_LUIGI_SPECIALHI, AS_LUIGI_SPECIALAIRHI, AS_LUIGI_SPECIALLW, and AS_LUIGI_SPECIALAIRLW.

Luigi special functions follow ftLuigi_Special<Move>_<Callback> and ftLuigi_SpecialAir<Move>_<Callback>, e.g. ftLuigi_SpecialN_StartAction, ftLuigi_SpecialAirN_Coll, ftLuigi_SpecialS_SetVars, ftLuigi_SpecialLw_UpdateRot.

Action-state flag macros use all-caps FTLUIGI_<SPECIAL>_<PURPOSE>_FLAG, such as FTLUIGI_SPECIALN_COLL_FLAG, FTLUIGI_SPECIALS_COLL_FLAG, FTLUIGI_SPECIALS_GROUND_AIR_FLAG, FTLUIGI_SPECIALS_MISFIRE_FLAG, and FTLUIGI_SPECIALLW_FLAG.

Special-attribute fields preserve offset prefixes and add move-scoped semantic names, such as x44_LUIGI_GREENMISSILE_MISFIRE_CHANCE, x50_LUIGI_SUPERJUMP_FREEFALL_MAX, and x70_LUIGI_CYCLONE_TAP_MOMENTUM.
Character-specific special-state fields can be upgraded from raw offset names to boolean semantic names, shown by SpecialAttrs_Luigi x222C_cycloneCharge.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Large assembly removal in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftluigi.s was paired with new C files and build-list additions, a standard partial-asm replacement tactic.
The new C includes decomp.me scratch links and original function addresses in comments, anchoring each callback to the assembly function it replaced.
Matching used explicit callback assignments, e.g. setting fp->cb.x21BC_callback_Accessory4 to ftLuigi_SpecialN_FireSpawn after Fireball state changes.
Ground/air transition callbacks preserve animation frames with fp->x894_currentAnimFrame in Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC calls.
The code uses ftcmd/throw flag checks such as x2200_ftcmd_var0 and x2210_ThrowFlags.b0/b3 to reproduce animation-command-triggered behavior.
Green Missile setup uses HSD_Randi against x44_LUIGI_GREENMISSILE_MISFIRE_CHANCE to match misfire RNG behavior.

Some match-oriented artifacts remain visible, such as temporary variables, inline helpers, explicit casts, and unusual expressions like '!fighter;' in fighter.c, suggesting code shape was prioritized for matching.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review approvals were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available; lessons are derived from title, file list, and diff evidence.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename Luigi special functions or clean up match artifacts such as temporary variables and '!fighter;' in fighter.c.
Search for subsequent changes to ftLuigiAttributes names to see which speculative comments were confirmed or corrected.
Search for later PRs touching func_8007CC78 to verify whether the Fighter* first signature remained stable.
Search for follow-up header cleanups around DemoMotionFiles and lbl_803D0AAC.
Search for later GameWatch header or ftgamewatch.s changes to understand why this Luigi PR also touched ftGameWatch files.
